e641e202-f1cc-4e6d-a8fb-5890fa762420.name: Update Tests
e641e202-f1cc-4e6d-a8fb-5890fa762420.prompt:
We are doing test-driven development for the project described in DESIGN_DOC.md. Currently we are focused on Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. Our focus right now is creating the test suite. All prior waves have been implemented, but Wave $1 has NOT been implemented, and will not be implemented until the test suite is complete.

Create a full test suite for Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. Tests should comprehensively cover all features listed for Wave $1. Tests should never skip and should never cheat. Tests should never replace properly testing functionality with placeholders or mocks or stubs or fallbacks or failsafes. Timeouts should be considered failures, and should be set reasonably to not time tests out prematurely. Tests should prioritize comprehensive end-to-end testing to the greatest extent possible. Some MCP plugins like Playwright are necessary for this, depending on the project.

The user's expectations after testing is for all waves through Wave $1 to be completely implemented. The tests should never betray these expectations by failing to test features properly or by cheating with placeholders or mocks somehow. (There are certain obvious exceptions like "visual look of real-time gameplay" that can't be tested, but these are the exception, not the norm.)

If ./test-audits/TEST_AUDIT_$1.{{turn}}.md exists, that means the test suite is partially implemented but has issues. Consult the audit and address these discrepancies.

The tests for Wave $1 should be runnable with a script `./run-tests.sh --wave=$1` that returns exit code 0 if all tests pass and nonzero exit code otherwise. The tests for ALL Waves should be runnable with just `./run-tests.sh` with no additional arguments, that returns exit code 0 if all tests pass and nonzero exit code otherwise.

While building these tests, you can reference ./how-to-be-a-good-coder, an organized encyclopedia of important knowledge specific to various coding topics. See ./how-to-be-a-good-coder/ABOUT.md for details.

For more information on features described in WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, see FEATURE_LIST_FULL.md

Don't run the tests yet. Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FEATURE_LIST_FULL.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./research, ./plans, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

7f90771b-9dac-48c8-bec6-543f00f8f19e.name: Audit Tests
7f90771b-9dac-48c8-bec6-543f00f8f19e.prompt:
We are doing test-driven development for the project described in DESIGN_DOC.md. Currently we are focused on Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. Our focus right now is creating the test suite. All prior waves have been implemented, but Wave $1 has NOT been implemented, and will not be implemented until the test suite is complete.

In theory, `./run-tests.sh --wave=$1` should run a test suite that comprehensively tests all features in Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, and `./run-tests.sh` should run a full test suite across all waves up to and including Wave $1.

Your job is to audit that the basic test-runner script does what's intended, and also audit the test suite for Wave $1 against the feature list in Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. Ensure that none of the tests skip or cheat. Ensure there are no mocks, placeholders, stubs, fallbacks, failsafes. Write your results to ./test-audits/TEST_AUDIT_$1.{{turn}}.md

Don't edit any code. Ultrathink.
